tickets.title: US001 (Allergen Filtering)
tickets.description:
As a: Student with a food allergy
I want to: set allergy preferences to hide dangerous dishes
So that: I can order safely without anxiety.

- [ ] Pass: Provides allergy checkboxes (e.g., 'No Peanuts') in settings.
- [ ] Pass: Instantly hides matching items from the menu.
- [ ] Pass: Hidden items do not appear in search results.

analysis.ambiguity1: The push notification request is ambiguous. It doesn't specify if 'students' means all users or just opted-in ones. It also lacks details on how the discount is calculated (fixed vs percentage) and what action occurs when tapped.
analysis.ambiguity2: The offline requirement is vague. It doesn't define data reconciliation rules: if the kitchen marks an item 'Sold Out' while offline, how does the system prevent mobile users from ordering it? Does the mobile app queue orders or block them?
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: Chef needs instant 'Sold Out' sync, but IT says Wi-Fi drops for up to 10 mins.
Solution: Make the Kitchen Display a PWA caching data in IndexedDB. If offline, the mobile app detects connection loss and warns users of delays. The system uses Predictive Thresholding (auto-marking items sold out at 5 units remaining) to prevent overselling. Upon reconnecting, logs sync, and any unfulfillable orders are automatically refunded.
analysis.nfr1: The Kitchen Display System must maintain local state using IndexedDB to prevent crashes during network drops, syncing queue states within 5s of reconnection.
analysis.nfr2: The system must dynamically filter the menu to hide allergen-containing items based on user settings, rendering changes in <100ms.
analysis.nfr3: Finalized mobile orders must transmit to the Kitchen Display within 3.0 seconds max latency, triggering an auditory alert.
